title: Perpetual vs Periodic — The Entry Sets
event: Hollis Co. purchases $8,000 of merchandise on account, then sells goods for $5,000 that cost $3,000. Prepare the entries under the assumed system.

lines.label: real-time record
lines.why: Perpetual runs everything through the Inventory account, live.
lines.trap: No Purchases account exists under perpetual — its appearance is the system tell.

lines.label: on account
lines.trap: Same credit either system — the debit side carries the difference.

lines.label: retail
lines.trap: Retail in entry one, cost in entry two — never mixed.

lines.label: earned
lines.trap: Identical under both systems — revenue recognition doesn't care about inventory records.

lines.label: cost out now
lines.why: Perpetual books the cost the moment goods leave.
lines.trap: This immediate COGS entry is exactly what periodic OMITS until closing.

lines.label: off the shelf
lines.why: The running record drops in real time.
lines.trap: At cost, never retail — crediting 5,000 here corrupts the account.

lines.label: temporary holding
lines.why: Periodic parks purchases in a temporary account; Inventory sleeps until the count.
lines.trap: The Inventory account shows the BEGINNING balance all year under periodic.

lines.label: on account
lines.trap: The debit account is the entire difference at purchase.

lines.label: retail
lines.trap: One entry, not two — periodic doesn't know the cost yet.

lines.label: earned
lines.trap: COGS waits for period-end: beginning + purchases − ending count. Adding a cost entry here is the giveaway error.

memorize.body: Periodic COGS = beginning inventory + net purchases − ending inventory (by count). Perpetual knows COGS at every sale.
Fastest system check on an exam: does the entry set include a Purchases account (periodic) or a COGS-at-sale entry (perpetual)?

questions.prompt: Under PERIODIC, what cost of goods sold is recorded at the moment of the $5,000 sale?

distractors.feedback: That's the perpetual move. Periodic computes ALL of COGS at period-end from the count — nothing at the sale.

build.scaffold: Pick the system first. Perpetual: Inventory account, two-entry sales. Periodic: Purchases account, one-entry sales.
